annotations.label: Platonic solid

anchor.snippet: a Platonic solid is a convex

annotations.note: Mathlib has the `Convex` predicate but no definition of a Platonic solid or regular convex polyhedron.

annotations.label: Regular polyhedron

anchor.snippet: Being a regular polyhedron means that the faces are congruent

annotations.note: No notion of polyhedron, regular polygon faces, or face/vertex regularity is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Exactly five Platonic solids

anchor.snippet: There are only five such polyhedra

annotations.note: No classification theorem for Platonic solids exists; only unproven Coxeter matrices A₃/B₃/H₃ for their symmetry groups appear.

annotations.label: Tetrahedron vertices half of cube

anchor.snippet: the vertices of the tetrahedron represent half of those of the cube

annotations.note: Mathlib has no coordinate models of the tetrahedron or cube.

annotations.label: Dodecahedron vertices shared with cube

anchor.snippet: Eight of the vertices of the dodecahedron are shared with the cube

annotations.note: No coordinate models of the dodecahedron or cube are present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Combinatorial characterization of Platonic solids

anchor.snippet: A convex polyhedron is a Platonic solid if and only if all three of the following requirements are met

annotations.note: No combinatorial polyhedron structure or Platonic-solid characterization exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Schläfli symbol

anchor.snippet: called the Schläfli symbol

annotations.note: Grep finds no Schläfli symbol definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Edge incidence relation pF=2E=qV

anchor.snippet: Since any edge joins two vertices and has two adjacent faces

annotations.note: No face/edge/vertex incidence counting for polyhedra is formalized.

annotations.label: Euler's formula

anchor.snippet: given by Euler's formula

annotations.note: The polyhedral formula V−E+F=2 is absent; Mathlib's `EulerCharacteristic` is the unrelated homological-algebra notion.

annotations.label: V, E, F determined by p and q

anchor.snippet: completely determine V , E , and F

annotations.note: No formulas expressing vertex/edge/face counts from a Schläfli symbol exist in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Swapping p and q interchanges F and V

anchor.snippet: Swapping p and q interchanges F and V

annotations.note: Polyhedral duality at the combinatorial level is not formalized.

annotations.label: Only five convex regular polyhedra exist

anchor.snippet: only five convex regular polyhedra exist

annotations.note: No classification proof of the regular convex polyhedra is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Angle defect

anchor.snippet: is called an angle defect

annotations.note: No notion of angular defect at a vertex of a polyhedron is defined.

annotations.label: Vertex angle sum less than 360°

anchor.snippet: must be strictly less than 360°

annotations.note: No statement bounding the sum of face angles at a polyhedron vertex exists.

annotations.label: Only five possibilities for {p,q}

anchor.snippet: there are only five possibilities for { p , q }

annotations.note: The enumeration of admissible Schläfli pairs is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dihedral angle

anchor.snippet: The dihedral angle is the interior angle between any two face planes

annotations.note: Mathlib's `dihedral` refers to dihedral groups; no dihedral angle between planes is defined.

annotations.label: Dihedral angle formula

anchor.snippet: of the solid { p , q } is given by the formula

annotations.note: No formula for the dihedral angle of a Platonic solid is present.

annotations.label: Coxeter number

anchor.snippet: called the Coxeter number

annotations.note: Grep finds no Coxeter number defined in Mathlib despite the Coxeter-group infrastructure.

annotations.label: Angular deficiency

anchor.snippet: The angular deficiency at the vertex of a polyhedron is the difference between the sum of the face-angles

annotations.note: No angular deficiency at a polyhedron vertex is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Defect formula

anchor.snippet: The defect, δ , at any vertex of the Platonic solids

annotations.note: No closed-form vertex-defect formula for Platonic solids is formalized.

annotations.label: Descartes' total angular defect theorem

anchor.snippet: By a theorem of Descartes

annotations.note: Descartes' theorem on total angular defect (=4π) is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Solid angle

anchor.snippet: The three-dimensional analog of a plane angle is a solid angle

annotations.note: Grep finds no definition of solid angle in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Vertex solid angle formula

anchor.snippet: at the vertex of a Platonic solid is given in terms of the dihedral angle

annotations.note: No solid-angle-at-a-vertex formula for Platonic solids is formalized.

annotations.label: Solid angle of a face

anchor.snippet: The solid angle of a face subtended from the center of a platonic solid is equal to the solid angle of a full sphere

annotations.note: No formula for the solid angle subtended by a face is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Three concentric spheres

anchor.snippet: the Platonic solids all possess three concentric spheres

annotations.note: The circum-, mid-, and insphere of a Platonic solid are not jointly formalized as concentric spheres.

annotations.label: Circumradius, midradius, inradius

anchor.snippet: are called the circumradius , the midradius , and the inradius

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `circumradius`/`inradius` for affine simplices generally, but no midradius and no Platonic-solid-specific radii.

annotations.label: Circumradius and inradius formulas

anchor.snippet: The circumradius R and the inradius r of the solid { p , q } with edge length a are given by

annotations.note: No closed-form circumradius/inradius formula in terms of {p,q} and edge length exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Midradius formula

anchor.snippet: The midradius ρ is given by

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of midradius nor any formula for it.

annotations.label: Circumradius/inradius ratio symmetric in p,q

anchor.snippet: The ratio of the circumradius to the inradius is symmetric in p and q

annotations.note: No such symmetry statement about Platonic-solid radii is formalized.

annotations.label: Surface area formula

anchor.snippet: The surface area , A , of a Platonic solid { p , q } is easily computed as area of a regular p -gon

annotations.note: No regular-polygon area or Platonic-solid surface area formula exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Volume formula

anchor.snippet: The volume is computed as F times the volume of the pyramid

annotations.note: No Platonic-solid volume formula is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Best sphere approximation

anchor.snippet: either the dodecahedron or the icosahedron may be seen as the best approximation to the sphere

annotations.note: No sphericity/best-sphere-approximation result for Platonic solids is present.

annotations.label: Distance identity for all five solids

anchor.snippet: For all five Platonic solids, we have

annotations.note: No vertex-distance identity for Platonic solids is formalized.

anchors.snippet: For all five Platonic solids, we have

annotations.label: Distances from circumscribed sphere point

anchor.snippet: If d i are the distances from the n vertices of the Platonic solid to any point on its circumscribed sphere

annotations.note: No sum-of-distance-powers identity over polyhedron vertices exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Rupert property

anchor.snippet: is said to have the Rupert property if a polyhedron of the same or larger size

annotations.note: The Rupert property is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: All Platonic solids are Rupert

anchor.snippet: All five Platonic solids have this property

annotations.note: No Rupert-property theorem for Platonic solids is formalized.

annotations.label: Dual polyhedron

anchor.snippet: Every polyhedron has a dual

annotations.note: Mathlib has convex cone duality but no dual-polyhedron construction.

annotations.label: Dual of a Platonic solid is Platonic

anchor.snippet: The dual of every Platonic solid is another Platonic solid

annotations.note: No statement about duals of Platonic solids is formalized.

annotations.label: Tetrahedron is self-dual

anchor.snippet: The tetrahedron is self-dual

annotations.note: No self-duality statement for the tetrahedron exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cube–octahedron dual pair

anchor.snippet: The cube and the octahedron form a dual pair

annotations.note: Only a docstring in `CoxeterMatrix.B` mentions the hypercube/orthoplex dual pair; no formal statement exists.

annotations.label: Dodecahedron–icosahedron dual pair

anchor.snippet: The dodecahedron and the icosahedron form a dual pair

annotations.note: No formal dodecahedron–icosahedron duality statement exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dual Schläfli symbol

anchor.snippet: then its dual has the symbol { q , p }

annotations.note: No Schläfli symbol or its dual is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Radii relation under dualization

anchor.snippet: The radii ( R , ρ , r ) of a solid and those of its dual

annotations.note: No radius relation between a Platonic solid and its dual is formalized.

annotations.label: Symmetry group of a polyhedron

anchor.snippet: Every polyhedron has an associated symmetry group

annotations.note: Mathlib has no polyhedron object and hence no general 'symmetry group of a polyhedron' definition, though Coxeter groups realize specific cases.

annotations.label: Order of the symmetry group

anchor.snippet: The order of the symmetry group is the number of symmetries of the polyhedron

annotations.note: No symmetry group of a polyhedron is defined, so its order is not formalized.

annotations.label: Regularity via uniformity

anchor.snippet: a polyhedron is regular if and only if it is vertex-uniform

annotations.note: No notions of vertex/edge/face uniformity or regularity of polyhedra exist in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Only three symmetry groups

anchor.snippet: There are only three symmetry groups associated with the Platonic solids

annotations.note: Mathlib defines the three Coxeter matrices A₃ (tetrahedral), B₃ (octahedral), and H₃ (icosahedral), but proves nothing about their being the complete set of Platonic symmetry groups.

annotations.label: Orders of rotation groups

anchor.snippet: The orders of the proper (rotation) groups are 12, 24, and 60

annotations.note: The rotation-group orders 12/24/60 of the polyhedral groups are not computed in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Central symmetry except tetrahedron

anchor.snippet: All Platonic solids except the tetrahedron are centrally symmetric

annotations.note: Point reflections exist in Mathlib but no central-symmetry statement about Platonic solids is formalized.

annotations.label: Kepler–Poinsot polyhedra

anchor.snippet: There exist four regular polyhedra that are not convex

annotations.note: The Kepler–Poinsot polyhedra are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cuboctahedron and icosidodecahedron

anchor.snippet: The next most regular convex polyhedra after the Platonic solids are the cuboctahedron

annotations.note: These quasiregular solids are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Archimedean solids

anchor.snippet: the thirteen Archimedean solids , which are the convex uniform polyhedra with polyhedral symmetry

annotations.note: Archimedean solids are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Catalan solids

anchor.snippet: they are two of the thirteen Catalan solids

annotations.note: Catalan solids are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Uniform polyhedra

anchor.snippet: The uniform polyhedra form a much broader class of polyhedra

annotations.note: Uniform polyhedra are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Johnson solids

anchor.snippet: The Johnson solids are convex polyhedra which have regular faces but are not uniform

annotations.note: Johnson solids are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Convex deltahedra

anchor.snippet: five of the eight convex deltahedra , which have identical, regular faces

annotations.note: Convex deltahedra are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Platonic solids as sphere tessellations

anchor.snippet: one can view the Platonic solids as regular tessellations of the sphere

annotations.note: Mathlib has tiling infrastructure but no regular tessellations of the sphere or their link to Platonic solids.

annotations.label: Hosohedra and dihedra

anchor.snippet: the hosohedra , {2, n } with 2 vertices at the poles

annotations.note: Hosohedra and dihedra are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sphere tessellation condition

anchor.snippet: Every regular tessellation of the sphere is characterized by a pair of integers

annotations.note: No characterization of regular spherical tessellations exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Plane tessellation condition

anchor.snippet: a regular tessellation of the plane is characterized by the condition

annotations.note: No characterization of regular plane tessellations (1/p+1/q=1/2) is formalized.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic tessellation condition

anchor.snippet: one can consider regular tessellations of the hyperbolic plane

annotations.note: No characterization of regular hyperbolic tessellations is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Polyhedra generalize to polytopes

anchor.snippet: polyhedra generalize to polytopes

annotations.note: Mathlib mentions polytopes only in passing (convex sets, grades) and has no general polytope definition.

annotations.label: Six convex regular 4-polytopes

anchor.snippet: There are exactly six of these figures

annotations.note: No classification of regular 4-polytopes exists; only the symmetry-group Coxeter matrices F₄/H₄ are defined, without proof.

annotations.label: Three convex regular polytopes above dimension four

anchor.snippet: there are only three convex regular polytopes

annotations.note: No classification of regular polytopes in dimension ≥5 is formalized, though Coxeter matrices Aₙ/Bₙ for their symmetry groups exist.
